name: Hind (Wife of Yazid)
title: Lioness of Umayyad Palace
shortDescription: The wife of Yazid ibn Mu'awiya who, upon seeing Imam Hussain's (AS) holy head in the Sham palace, shouted at Yazid and disgraced him.
fullStory:
Hind bint Abdullah (or Hind bint Amr) was the wife of Yazid ibn Mu'awiya. Before marrying Yazid, she had grown up in the house of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) and harbored a hidden devotion to the Prophet's (PBUH) family.

When the captives of Karbala and the holy heads of the martyrs were brought to Yazid's palace in Sham, Yazid was intoxicated with victory. At one moment, hearing the wailing of the Ahl al-Bayt women and seeing Imam Hussain's (AS) holy head, Hind lost her patience.

Being behind the curtain, she threw a chador over her head and rushed into Yazid's male gathering. In front of everyone, she shouted at Yazid: 'O Yazid! Is this the head of Hussain, the son of Fatima, daughter of the Messenger of God?'

Yazid, surprised and embarrassed by his wife's action, stood up, covered her, and to acquit himself, blamed Ibn Ziyad. With this brave move, Hind shattered the false legitimacy of Yazid's victory right inside his own palace.
quoteArabic: يا يَزِيدُ! أَرَأْسُ الحُسَيْنِ ابْنِ فاطِمَةَ بِنْتِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ؟...
quote: (Her shout in the palace:) O Yazid! Is this the head of Hussain, the son of Fatima, daughter of the Messenger of God?
